single_gpu.note: model state_dict + optimizer state + iter_num all bitwise-equal between A-path (train 10 -> save -> resume -> 10 more) and B-path (train 20 straight).

ddp_4rank.note: With strict-determinism flags (see fixes_landed), A-vs-B resume drift at iter 20 is 3e-5 — near the bf16 representation floor. Two fresh DDP runs from same seed remain bitwise-equal, confirming DDP training itself is deterministic. Remaining 3e-5 drift is residual NCCL reduction-order noise in bf16 and is not expected to be eliminable without fp32 residual stream. Downgraded from 'open investigation' — see 7485-step retrain alignment doc for end-to-end validation.

fixes_landed: per-rank RNG + seq_data_pos + prefetched X/Y via dist.all_gather_object
per-rank restore from _per_rank[rank] slot on resume
strict-determinism env: NCCL_ALGO=Ring, NCCL_PROTO=Simple, NCCL_P2P_DISABLE=1, NCCL_NVLS_ENABLE=0, NCCL_COLLNET_ENABLE=0, NVIDIA_TF32_OVERRIDE=0, matmul.allow_tf32=False, cudnn.allow_tf32=False
